question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockAssembler.append()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send many small BlockItemSets each just below the maxBlockItems limit, then send one more that pushes pendingCount over the limit, causing a BlockStreamException that discards all accumulated pending items and prevents gossip of all transactions in that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause all configured block nodes to return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE from getBlockRange() simultaneously (e.g., by making all nodes return firstAvailableBlock == -1), so that hasBlock() returns false for every node in both the active and inactive loops, causing getNode() to throw BlockStreamException and preventing gossip of all transactions indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a block with a previousHash that does not match the last verified block's hash, causing verifyHashChain() to throw HashMismatchException, which propagates through onBlockStream() and prevents gossip of all transactions in that block and all subsequent blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifyBlockNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a block whose BlockHeader number does not match the expected consecutive block number (lastBlockNumber + 1), causing verifyBlockNumber() to throw InvalidStreamFileException, preventing gossip of all transactions in that block and stalling the entire block stream?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifySignature()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a block with an invalid or missing TSS signature, causing verifyTssSignature() to throw an exception, preventing gossip of all transactions in that block and triggering onError() to eventually mark the node inactive?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node hold the gRPC stream open indefinitely without sending any response, causing grpcCall.read() to block until the responseTimeout deadline, then throw a StatusRuntimeException, triggering onError() and preventing gossip of all transactions in the requested block range for the duration of the timeout?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockAssembler.onEndOfBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send multiple EndOfBlock messages for the same block number, causing the second call to find pending empty and invoke handleRecoverableError(), potentially causing the block to be processed twice or not at all, leading to duplicate or missing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger BlockNodeDiscoveryService.onRegisteredNodeChanged() repeatedly to keep the cache in CLEARED state, causing getBlockNodes() to repeatedly query the database and rebuild the node list, creating a sustained window where nodes are being closed and recreated and no blocks can be streamed, preventing gossip of transactions during that window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a SubscribeStreamResponse with BLOCK_ITEMS case but with a BlockItemSet containing items where the first item has ItemCase UNSET or an unexpected case, causing append() to throw BlockStreamException('Incorrect first block item case'), preventing gossip of all transactions in that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onError()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send exactly (maxSubscribeAttempts - 1) errors, keeping the node in a degraded but active state, then send a valid block with a targeted transaction omitted, so the subscriber accepts the incomplete block and the omitted transaction is never gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: hasBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node return a block range where lowerEndpoint() is set to a future block number far beyond the current chain tip, causing hasBlock() to set nextBlockNumber to that future value and return true, causing streamBlocks() to request a non-existent block and fail, preventing gossip of all transactions in the actual next block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockAssembler.timeout()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send the first BlockItemSet (starting the stopwatch) and then delay subsequent items until timeout.toMillis() - stopwatch.elapsed() becomes zero or negative, causing grpcCall.read(0, MILLISECONDS) to return null immediately, silently aborting the stream and preventing gossip of all transactions in the partially received block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodesChanged() property-equality check to inject a node with the same host/port/TLS settings but a different internal state (e.g., manipulated block range), bypassing node recreation and causing the subscriber to continue using a stale or malicious node that omits targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a SubscribeStreamResponse with BLOCK_ITEMS where the BlockItemSet contains a BLOCK_HEADER item with a block number that is not the expected next block number, causing verifyBlockNumber() to throw InvalidStreamFileException after assembly, preventing gossip of all transactions in that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: drainGrpcBuffer()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node cause drainGrpcBuffer() to block indefinitely in the while(grpcCall.read() != null) loop by continuously sending data on the cancelled call, tying up the single-thread executor and preventing future drain tasks from running, leading to gRPC memory exhaustion that crashes the importer and halts all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a scenario where all active nodes return false from hasBlock() (block not in their range) and all inactive nodes also return false, so getNode() throws BlockStreamException, and since the exception is not caught in doGet(), the entire block streaming cycle fails and all transactions in the target block are never gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockAssembler.onBlockItemSet()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a BlockItemSet where blockItems.getFirst().getItemCase() returns BLOCK_HEADER but the actual BlockHeader proto is empty/malformed, causing blockStreamReader.read() to fail during onBlockStream(), preventing gossip of all transactions in that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a valid block stream for block N but with all transaction items removed (only BLOCK_HEADER and block proof items), causing the block to be assembled and verified successfully but with zero transactions, effectively suppressing gossip of all transactions that should have been in block N?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a wrapped record block with an empty recordFileSignaturesList, causing verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature() to throw InvalidStreamFileException('No record file signatures'), preventing gossip of all transactions in that wrapped block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlockRange()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node return a ServerStatus response where firstAvailableBlock > lastAvailableBlock, causing Range.closed() to throw an IllegalArgumentException, which is caught and returns EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE, making the node appear to have no blocks and preventing it from being selected to serve the target block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: BlockAssembler.onEndOfBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a valid block stream but with the EndOfBlock.blockNumber set to a value different from the BlockHeader.number, causing handleRecoverableError() to be called, and if the implementation throws, preventing gossip of all transactions in that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: streamBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node send a gRPC stream that abruptly closes (RST_STREAM) after delivering only partial block items, causing grpcCall.read() to throw a StatusRuntimeException, triggering onError() and preventing gossip of all transactions in the partially received block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getBlockNodes() closes old nodes (currentNodes.forEach(BlockNode::close)) before the new nodes are set, creating a brief window where nodes.get() contains closed channels, causing streamBlocks() to fail with a channel-already-shutdown error and preventing gossip of transactions during that window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user controlling a block node cause node.streamBlocks() to throw an unchecked exception other than BlockStreamException (e.g., OutOfMemoryError from a huge block), bypassing the onError() call in BlockNode and leaving the error counter unchanged, so the node is never marked inactive and continues to serve malicious blocks that suppress targeted transactions?
